title: French Chicken Rillettes
description: A French-inspired dish featuring chicken rillettes made with flavorful chicken and served with crispy, snappy accompaniments.
instructions: Season the chicken thighs with salt and pepper.
In a large Dutch oven, melt 1 tablespoon of butter over medium heat. Add the sliced onion and cook until caramelized, about 20-25 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add the chicken thighs and cook until browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the flour and cook for 1 minute.
Add the white wine and chicken broth. Stir to combine, scraping up any browned bits.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Cook the chicken for 2-1/2 to 3 hours, or until it's tender and easily shreds with a fork.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Using two forks, shred the chicken into bite-sized pieces.
Stir in the remaining 1 tablespoon of butter and the chopped fresh thyme.
Serve the chicken rillettes with crusty bread for dipping.
